questions.question_text: How many hours of pre-licensing education must an Arizona salesperson candidate complete?
questions.correct_answer: 90 hours plus a 6-hour contract writing course
distractors: 90 hours with no additional courses required
120 hours plus a separate 6-hour ethics course
60 hours plus a 12-hour contract writing course
questions.explanation: Arizona requires 90 hours of salesperson pre-licensing education from an ADRE-approved school PLUS a separate 6-hour Contract Writing Course, totaling 96 hours before license application.

questions.question_text: A salesperson candidate has completed the 90-hour pre-licensing course. What additional course must they finish before applying for a license?
questions.correct_answer: A 6-hour Contract Writing Course
questions.explanation: Arizona requires completion of both the 90-hour pre-licensing course AND a 6-hour Contract Writing Course before a candidate can submit their license application to ADRE.

questions.question_text: An aspiring broker already holds an Arizona salesperson license. How many additional education hours are required for a broker license?
questions.correct_answer: 90 additional hours
questions.explanation: Arizona broker candidates need 90 additional hours beyond their salesperson education (180 total), plus 3 years of active salesperson experience within the previous 5 years.

questions.question_text: Where must Arizona pre-licensing courses be taken to qualify for the exam?
questions.correct_answer: An ADRE-approved school
questions.explanation: Pre-licensing education must be completed through an ADRE-approved education provider. Not all universities or community colleges are automatically approved — the school must have specific ADRE approval.

questions.question_text: A broker candidate has been licensed as a salesperson for 2 years. Can they apply for a broker license?
questions.correct_answer: No, 3 years of active experience is required
distractors: Yes, if they complete 90 additional hours
Yes, 2 years is the minimum requirement
No, 5 years of experience is required
questions.explanation: Arizona requires 3 years of active salesperson experience within the previous 5 years for broker licensure. Two years is insufficient regardless of additional education.

questions.question_text: What is the minimum age to obtain an Arizona real estate salesperson license?
questions.correct_answer: 18 years old
questions.explanation: Arizona requires salesperson candidates to be at least 18 years of age. This is the standard minimum age in most states for real estate licensure.

questions.question_text: What type of background check does Arizona require for license applicants?
questions.correct_answer: Fingerprint clearance card through DPS
questions.explanation: Arizona requires applicants to obtain a fingerprint clearance card through the Department of Public Safety (DPS). This is more rigorous than a simple self-disclosure or county check.

questions.question_text: A license applicant has a prior felony conviction. How does ADRE handle the application?
questions.correct_answer: ADRE may deny the application based on the record
distractors: All felons are permanently barred from licensure
Felonies are only relevant if within 5 years
ADRE cannot consider criminal history at all
questions.explanation: ADRE can deny license applications based on criminal history, particularly felonies involving moral turpitude. However, a felony does not result in an automatic permanent bar — each case is reviewed individually.

questions.question_text: An applicant who is not a U.S. citizen wants to obtain an Arizona real estate license. Is this possible?
questions.correct_answer: Yes, if they have legal presence in the U.S.
distractors: No, only U.S. citizens may apply for licensure
Only with a special waiver granted by ADRE
Yes, but only as a temporary provisional license
questions.explanation: Arizona does not require U.S. citizenship for licensure. Applicants must be legal residents of the United States or have legal presence, which includes permanent residents and certain visa holders.

questions.question_text: An applicant passes the national portion of the exam but fails the state portion. What is the result?
questions.correct_answer: They must retake only the failed state portion
distractors: They must retake both portions from scratch
They receive a provisional license for 6 months
The national score is averaged with the state
questions.explanation: Arizona's split exam scores each section independently. If a candidate passes one section but fails the other, they only need to retake the failed section, not both.

questions.question_text: Every real estate brokerage firm in Arizona must have which type of licensee?
questions.correct_answer: A designated broker
questions.explanation: Arizona law requires every brokerage firm to have a designated broker who is responsible for all real estate activities conducted by the firm and its licensees.

questions.question_text: An LLC wants to operate a real estate brokerage in Arizona. What license must the LLC obtain?
questions.correct_answer: An entity license with a designated broker
distractors: A corporate broker license without a designee
Individual licenses for all LLC members
A special ADRE business operations permit
questions.explanation: Corporations, LLCs, and partnerships must hold an entity license and designate a licensed broker as their designated broker. The entity itself must be licensed in addition to the individual broker.

questions.question_text: A newly licensed salesperson wants to work independently without a broker. Is this permitted in Arizona?
questions.correct_answer: No, a salesperson must work under a designated broker
distractors: Yes, after completing 6 months of practice
Yes, with an independent contractor agreement
No, unless they obtain a provisional broker license
questions.explanation: Arizona salespersons must be employed by a designated broker at all times. They cannot operate independently. Only licensed brokers may work without supervision from another broker.

questions.question_text: What distinguishes a broker license from a salesperson license in Arizona?
questions.correct_answer: A broker can operate independently or employ others
distractors: A broker handles only commercial real estate sales
A broker is exempt from continuing education rules
A broker does not need any oversight from ADRE
questions.explanation: The key distinction is that brokers can operate independently and employ or supervise salespersons. Salespersons must always work under a designated broker. Both license types require CE and are subject to ADRE oversight.

questions.question_text: A designated broker retires. What must the brokerage firm do?
questions.correct_answer: Appoint a new designated broker promptly
distractors: Continue operations for up to 90 days
Notify clients but no other action
questions.explanation: Every firm must have a designated broker at all times. If the designated broker leaves, the firm must promptly appoint a qualified replacement. Operating without a designated broker violates Arizona law.

questions.question_text: How often must Arizona real estate licensees renew their licenses?
questions.correct_answer: Every 2 years
questions.explanation: Arizona real estate licenses must be renewed every 2 years (biennially) from the date of original issuance. Salespersons need 24 hours CE; designated brokers need 30 hours.

questions.question_text: How many CE hours must an Arizona salesperson complete per renewal period?
questions.correct_answer: 24 hours every 2 years
questions.explanation: Arizona salespersons and associate brokers must complete 24 hours of CE per 2-year renewal period. Designated brokers require 30 hours. As of 2025, mandatory topics include Firewise, Deed Fraud, and Arizona Water.

questions.question_text: A licensee's license expired 14 months ago. What must they do to reactivate?
questions.correct_answer: Retake the licensing exam
distractors: Pay a late fee and complete CE
questions.explanation: If a license has been expired for more than 1 year, the licensee must retake and pass the licensing exam. Late renewal with a penalty is only available during the first year after expiration.

questions.question_text: Which special course must designated brokers complete as part of their continuing education?
questions.correct_answer: The Broker Management Clinic
questions.explanation: Designated brokers have a specific CE requirement to complete the Broker Management Clinic, which covers supervision responsibilities, trust account management, and compliance obligations unique to the designated broker role.

questions.question_text: A salesperson's license expired 6 months ago. They want to reactivate. What is required?
questions.correct_answer: Late renewal with a penalty fee and CE
questions.explanation: Within the first year of expiration, licensees can renew late by paying a penalty fee and completing any outstanding continuing education. After 1 year of expiration, the exam must be retaken.
